svg.text: AMHARIC + OROMO — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 31D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— AMHARIC — PASSAGE 1 · ETHIOPIAN PROVERB —
አመሳ ለካዕኙ ያስሳጣል።
əməsa lək'aNətu yasəsatəal.
"A fish is caught because of its mouth." — Be careful what you say.
— AMHARIC — PASSAGE 2 · MOTTO —
ኢትዮጵያ ለአለም
Ityopy'a ləaləm
"Ethiopia forever." — Traditional patriotic expression.
— AMHARIC — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
ይህ የተሳነው መልእክት በነጳ ተዸኝነውነት ለዘክት ተደረገ።
Yəh yətəsaNəwə mələəkt bənəts'ina təzəɨwənəwənt ləzəkt tədərəgə.
This message freely who-remembered forward was-made.
"This message was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN AMHARIC —
wədəayt: "future" — lit. "the-forward-direction." From wəd (toward) + ayt (direction).
Amharic orients time spatially: what is ahead (wəd) = the future; what is behind = the past.
bənəts'ina: "freely" — from nətsʼ (freedom) — the gift is etymologically rooted in liberty.
OROMO (AFAAN OROMOO) · RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— OROMO — PASSAGE 1 · OROMO PROVERB —
Namni nama tolcha.
Person person builds.
"A person builds a person." — We are built by each other. Community is the foundation.
— OROMO — PASSAGE 2 · PROVERB —
Ollaan ollaa barbaada.
Neighbor neighbor seeks.
"A neighbor seeks a neighbor." — Humans are social beings who need one another.
— OROMO — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
Kun isiniif bilisaan, namoota fuuldura isin yaadataniin, hojjetame.
This for-you freely, people forward you remembered-by, was-made.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FORWARD" IN OROMO —
fuuldura: "forward" — lit. "in front of the face." From fuul (face) + dura (before/in-front).
Oromo conceptualizes the future as what stands before you — visible, ahead, faced directly.
bilisaan: "freely" — from bilisaa (free). Root shared with bilisummaa (freedom).
yaadataniin: "who remembered" — from yaadachuu (to remember) + -aniin (relative clause marker).
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
Amharic: official language of Ethiopia, ~35M native speakers. Written in Ethiopic/Fidel abugida.
Oromo: largest language in Ethiopia, ~40M native speakers. Written in Latin-based Qubee alphabet.
Both are Afro-Asiatic languages but from different branches: Amharic = Semitic; Oromo = Cushitic.
Both are SOV. Both have ejective consonants. Both originate in the same highland region.
See plates 31A–31C for scripts, phonology, and grammar. Plate 34A–34D covers Tigrinya (related to Amharic).
PLATE 31D ALT · AMHARIC / OROMO · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
